title: 7.2.3 Foundation Rocking
content:
7.2.3 Foundation Rocking
Rocking of a wall and its foundation on the supporting
oil has been observed in the field. Though recognized
s a potentially favorable mode of nonlinear response
nd a source of damping rather than significant damage,
xcessive rocking could theoretically lead to some
nstability and nonstructural damage in the
uperstructure, particularly if various walls rock out-of-
hase or elements attached to the walls cannot tolerate
he wall drift. See FEMA 273 and ATC-40 for an
valuation methodology for foundation rocking.
